summary: Drew DeVault announces that SourceHut now offers full first-class Mercurial support, on par with its existing git offering. The integration includes CI support, email-driven collaboration, and is fully open source and self-hostable. The work was largely driven by community contributor Ludovic Chabant and tested against major repositories like mozilla-central and NetBSD src. DeVault positions SourceHut as filling a gap in the Mercurial ecosystem and signals openness to supporting other version control systems.
key_points: SourceHut's Mercurial support is now fully integrated with CI builds and email-based collaboration, matching git feature parity
The implementation was community-driven, with Mercurial contributor Ludovic Chabant doing instrumental work
Large-scale testing was done against mozilla-central and NetBSD src repositories, with NetBSD actively helping stress-test the platform
SourceHut invites other version control communities to reach out about potential platform support
money_quotes: Of course, it's also 100% free-as-in-freedom, open source software that you can deploy on your own servers.
There weren't a lot of good options for Mercurial fans before SourceHut. Let's fix that together!
SourceHut will be taking a more active role in the Hg community, just like we have for git, and together we'll build a great platform for software development.
Hg support on SourceHut was largely written by members of the Mercurial community.
If there are other version control communities interested in SourceHut support, please reach out!
key_insight: SourceHut differentiates itself by embracing version control diversity and community-driven development, offering Mercurial users a fully free, feature-complete platform where none previously existed.
